name: Foundations
what_happens: Hiring opens for the four senior leads; AI Platform vendor RFP issued and vendor selected. Configuration fixes begin (unblock the Help Centre robots.txt, fix the breach-response page, publish the agent-readability file). AI Platform v0 (gateway + vector store + first eval harness) goes to staging; audit-log core spec reviewed by DPO; EU AI Act Article 50 disclosure scaffolding ready. Structured-data labels shipped on key pages; the replacement chatbot's retrieval corpus is chunked and embedded.
success_state: Four leads in seat, platform v0 in staging, AI search visibility risen from 38 to 70–78 on configuration + structured-data work, EU AI Act Aug 2 2026 deadline cleared, chatbot corpus ready for closed alpha.

name: First ship
what_happens: The 7 IC hires close. Replacement customer-support chatbot goes closed-alpha then ships to the public surface. AI-callable surface v1 published in the public registry; ChatGPT App submitted then live. Pass-economics calculator reaches closed alpha then a beta cohort. Bilateral pilot conversations begin with DB, SBB, ÖBB, NS (the Phase 2 entry condition).
success_state: Three customer-facing AI features live (replacement chatbot, AI-callable interface in registry, ChatGPT App). AI search visibility 38 → 80–82. Public support deflection 30–40%. At least two operator pilot MoUs signed. Team at 11, platform in production. The breach-trust ceiling starts to lift because features ship with provenance from day one.

name: Federation pilots
what_happens: Cross-operator disruption pilot launches with the 4-operator cohort (DB, SBB, ÖBB, NS). Multi-country trip planner goes alpha → public release, replacing what the May 2026 sunset took down. AI-callable surface expands to 5–10 tools; pass-economics calculator full release. Post-trip closure bundle enters closed alpha. Youth-cohort compliance work (DPIA + special-category data flow) begins. Disruption pilot evaluated at 6 months for expansion.
success_state: Pilot running with 4 operators and graduated to an expand decision (6–8 operators). Trip planner in production. Post-trip closure live to public. AI search visibility 84–86. EU AI Act Aug 2027 obligations cleared.

name: Scale
what_happens: Disruption coverage scales to 8–12 operators. AI-callable surface matures to 12–15 tools. Personalisation Tier 1 (pass recommendation on stated trip intent) ships if the Xebia data-lake gate passed. Youth-cohort AI v1 ships in limited deployment (multilingual passport-replacement guidance for the breach-affected cohort).
success_state: Public support deflection 45–55% (calibrated to internal Zendesk's 50%+); Trustpilot recovering toward 4.1; federation moat converting at scale with disruption across 8–12 operators; AI search visibility 85–87. The Supervisory Board has issued an explicit AI mandate or opened the cooperative-level conversation with documented bilateral-pilot evidence. Layer 5 scoped for 2028+.
